title: Transaction Metadata
description: Transaction metadata is wallet-side context such as contacts, descriptions, tags, and notes attached to a payment or activity item. It helps users understand history and search their activity, but it is not stored on-chain and needs explicit portability if users change wallets.

resources.title: Bitcoin Design: Activity
resources.notes: Primary discussion of wallet activity presentation, recipient fields, and why metadata portability matters.

resources.title: Bitcoin Design: Contacts
resources.notes: Shows how wallets attach names, reusable payment endpoints, and contextual meaning to transactions without confusing that data for on-chain state.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for wallet behavior, transaction history, and the underlying payment model that this metadata decorates.
